I wished there was a refrigerator.

reviews.title: Extremely Disappointing Stay
reviews.positives: none
reviews.negatives:
Extremely Disappointing Stay

My experience at this hotel was by far the worst I’ve ever had, and it is absolutely not worth the over $300 per night price tag. Here’s a detailed account of my stay, which I hope will help others reconsider booking this property:
	1.	Room Issues:
	•	The air conditioning in my room was broken on the first day and constantly blew directly at my head, making it extremely uncomfortable. I contacted housekeeping multiple times, but no one came for hours. Eventually, I had to go to the front desk myself, where the staff was dismissive and simply told me to “keep waiting.”
	•	The bathroom ventilation was also broken, leaving unpleasant odors lingering in the room after use.
	•	During my three-day stay, I had to replace the hairdryer twice, and even then, the replacements didn’t work properly.
	2.	Poor Service:
	•	The front desk and staff were unhelpful and unaccommodating throughout my stay. Worse, I noticed clear discriminatory behavior: when I tried to speak with staff, they deliberately avoided me (an Asian guest) and instead prioritized serving white guests. This kind of treatment was both disappointing and unacceptable.
	3.	Hygiene Concerns:
	•	I found blood stains on the bedding, which was absolutely appalling.
	•	The room itself was small, dirty, and far below the standard one would expect at this price point.
	4.	Inconvenient Location:
	•	The hotel’s location is very remote and inconvenient, making it difficult to get anywhere. For travelers looking for easy access to the city, this is not an ideal choice.

This hotel failed to meet even the most basic standards of comfort, cleanliness, and service. As someone who has stayed in many hotels, this was my first time experiencing such poor treatment and conditions. I feel compelled to write this detailed review to warn others.

This was by far the worst hotel experience I’ve ever had, and I would strongly advise others to think twice before booking here.

reviews.title: It is very close and convenient to the Apollo Theater.
reviews.text: I stayed here to watch Fujii Kaze's piano concert at the Apollo Theater. It's really close! From the entrance facing the road, you go up to the 5th floor, which is the lounge area with a check-in counter where music is playing. The facilities were beautiful, with paintings on cushions and chairs. It seemed like there wasn't a clear sign at the counter? At first, I thought it might be the concierge desk, but I was right. The guidance was only in English, but it was kind and easy to understand. The elevator can only be used with a guest card key, so I think that's reassuring. The inside of the elevator and the guest rooms were very clean. If I had to say something, the toilet seat was cold and the curtains weren't blackout, but that didn't bother me. If you come to Harlem, although it is a bit pricey, I definitely want to recommend this hotel.

We could've stayed at the Doubletree and had a better experience that was worth the money. When you pay over $500 per night you expect more. The room was tiny and awkward. Another issue that occurred is that I was told by the same check-in person that I had 30 % off at the bar/restaurant. reviews.title: Very nice aesthetic but fails in operations ESPECIALLY accounting. Will not be returning!
reviews.positives: Hotel has a very nice aesthetic and some accommodating staff
reviews.negatives:
• The room did not have a mini fridge,  which I would figure would be a staple in 4+ star hotels. The staff did accommodate and provided one, but it was weird it wasn't already in there.

• The water was shut off one night without any notice, and the front desk seemed to be unaware of the issue. No apology was issued. They just expected us to deal with it.

• The room's curtains were powered by hydrolics, which were malfunctioning. The dark shade couldn't be moved up, resulting in a very dark room. I addressed room service the first night, but it was NEVER rectified.

• The hotel caters very much to a very rowdy party crowd, and there's a lot of going on in the lobby to the point it's a fire hazard with people crowding in front of the elevator. It takes a long time to get an elevator with the hotel reserving one of the elevators for their rooftop bar.

• The absolute WORST part is their accounting and how confusing and shady they bill you. Booking through a third party, I understand that the staff may have limited jurisdiction and information on the payment processing, but it was very clear that I was paying a specific price for the stay. I was billed for the entire stay up front with an additional security deposit during check-in. I had no problem with that as long as there were no surprise charges during or after the stay.. They randomly tried to bill me a random value in the middle of the night  of the night during my stay. I addressed it to the reception and accounting, and they were telling me that was normal. I've stayed at MANY hotels and can confirm that is NOT normal. I did not acquire any additional charges or bill anything to the room. Any additional charges should be addressed during check out.

It was suspicious that I was getting randomly billed. My bank did not approve that charge since I was not familiar with it. The value was very different from the rate of the stay and what I was billed.

reviews.title: Home Up in Harlem....Renaissance edition.
reviews.positives: I live in Harlem. I wanted to try this place because it's new. It was quite a sight to behold upon arrival, a radiant foursome of Rich Aunties awaiting their car before a night on the town. Doug E. Fresh was doing a photo shoot in the lobby. The ambiance of it is hip, fresh, and Harlem. All staff I encountered over the Xmas weekend was stellar. Room service breakfast was great. I ordered the Victoria, I believe. It was braised oxtails over an egg scramble and potatoes. Excellant! My room was changed without hesitation when I walked in and had an issue. Ms. Lambert at check-out was exceptional and has a perfect personality for almost anything, I'm sure. I really WANTED to adore what is supposed to be a win for Harlem, I guess. But I digress. The hotel has potential. I like the vibe. I would return again to dine. It was nice to "getaway" and still be able to do things on my home base.
reviews.negatives: Check-in is on the 5th floor... a bit annoyed that there is no way to know that. You arrive on the floor of the bustling restaurant and check in off to the side. When I arrived in my room, I was bewildered by the layout. The bathroom configuration has to be the most bazaar things I have seen in a hotel thus far. What in the children's art design contest was this? Room and bathroom are separated by the bathroom mirror. Dassit! Dassit, and that's all. The "door" is on a sliding track. Ok, cool.... but it stops where.... at the bathroom sink. There is literally no form or function, rhyme, or reason for this debacle of a setup. A fumble of sorts. There could not have been a walk through after this build where someone put a check mark next to this fumble. You can literally see someone on the toilet from any angle in the room. Is it jail? Jail, you say. My new room was much better. Full bathroom with the same style door, which swung but had no lock. It's not a deal breaker. Walls are thin. I heard everything in the next room. Obviously, it's was a bevy of soul sisters enjoying their weekend. I did love that for them. It wasn't unbearable once I settled in on my crime show with Tamron Hall.

3) do not put out stained towels or pillowcases 

Regarding service, it'd be great if they had a bellhop at the front entrance. As a party of women, it was difficult to load and unload our baggage and bring it up/down. I believe as a marriot hotel, they aim for 4star service so the amenities/service offerings should reflect that.

We were sent off to a different hotel in the middle of the night because the hotel was oversold. Needless to say that we were in disbelief about the situation and we had to leave for the night after traveling four hours, we were tired and to take an Uber with all the luggage we had. #1. Don’t promise benefits you don’t have.  We DID NOT RECEIVE THE FOLLOWING promised bonus:
- Uptown Hop Off / Hop On – NYC Top View Tours for 2 people (one time during stay) 
- Museum of the City of New York + Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum tickets for 2 people (one time during stay).  We asked about it twice and we’re told they were negotiating for these benefits but didn’t have them yet.  
#2. Cleaners did not put towels and coffee in the room everyday. They removed all used towels but didn’t replace them or the coffee service so everyday we had to call and have them delivered.  
#3.  Restaurant/ Bar needs to communicate its hours of service.  Put it in the advertised information and inform the Front desk.  The front desk thought it was open till midnight but most often it closed by 11:30PM.

reviews.title: Overall a pleasant time.
reviews.positives:
It's worth mentioning that this hotel has recently opened, so it's natural to expect a few flaws. However, despite the mishaps, the staff was incredibly accommodating and helpful. I had a pleasant experience with both my room and the staff. 

The location of the hotel was ideal, with all the necessary stores, restaurants, grocery options, and even fast food and liquor stores within a short walking distance. Additionally, it was conveniently close to the event I was attending. 

Given the circumstances, I would definitely consider returning to the hotel in the future, hoping that any necessary improvements have been made.
reviews.negatives:
We had dinner at The Victoria, and while the drinks exceeded our expectations, the food had a long wait time. Unfortunately, our experience was dampened when the waiter accidentally spilled a drink on my friend. It was not the best way to end a tiring day of traveling. However, the staff was very accommodating and tried their best to rectify the situation. 

In addition to the mishap at dinner, we also had to request a room change due to a horrible odor. It was truly horrendous, and I wish I was joking. To our surprise, even the room my sister was moved to had the same unpleasant scent. We didn't want to be a nuisance, especially since the hotel seemed to be fully booked. The smell was just unbearable.

On a positive note, my own room was a different story altogether. It was absolutely amazing. The views were breathtaking, there were no foul smells, and everything was impeccably clean. I would easily rate my room a 10/10.

reviews.title: This is a striking hotel with great energy and attentive staff
reviews.positives:
The Renaissance New York Harlem Hotel is beautifully designed and set in a landmark location, with excellent accessibility—six subway lines are within a five-minute walk.

The rooms are chic and thoughtfully laid out, featuring large, comfortable beds and floor-to-ceiling windows with sweeping views of the city or park. The TV setup is intuitive and allows for HDMI use, which is a nice touch. One subjective note: the toiletries provided were among the least appealing I’ve encountered at a hotel, so I would recommend bringing your own.

The overall atmosphere is vibrant and welcoming, and nearly all of our interactions—with both staff and other guests—were genuinely pleasant. At check-in, the front desk agent was especially helpful and promptly assisted us in switching rooms after we noticed an unusual odor in the initial room.
reviews.negatives: My only significant concern relates to safety preparedness. During our stay, my boyfriend sustained a minor cut, and when we contacted the front desk for a first-aid kit, we were informed that none were stocked. As someone who works in occupational safety, this stood out as an essential basic requirement regardless of hotel size or brand, and it was disappointing to encounter this oversight.

reviews.title: Experiential, Special and Comfortable
reviews.positives: The hotel itself was wonderfully expressive and unique, capturing the feel of an old theatre of which the building was once was and paying homage to classic NY jazz--It has character and my room was wonderfully cozy with nice details evoking the theme of the hotel included. The staff was generally excellent as well.
reviews.negatives: Very minor quibble--While the food was excellent and it was wonderful having the $25.00 voucher, the food was expensive--At the same time, this is understandable in a high scale hotel. A suggestion--perhaps it would be great to have a small snack/ souvenir store that usually exists in hotel--to grab after hours snacks or beverages without needing to order from restaurant. Or perhaps a snack vending machine would be great. They did have a beverages vending machine. I also noticed many families at the hotel and having a snack area for the kids would support accessibility for everyone.

reviews.negatives:
Hotel had a destination charge of $25 where you get $25 worth of food and beverages. This is fine but the tickets were problematic. Sometimes the servers lost track of the tickets. It would have been more efficient if the room was just credited $25 and the servers wouldn’t have to deal with paper tickets. Sometimes the lobby bar was full but they wouldn’t open up other sections and patrons were forced to share long tables. This is ok but unusual for this caliber of hotel.

Rooms did not have dressers. I can understand as room space is constrained already. It it would have been nice to have a dresser.
